prompt: My cell viability (MTT) assay is giving me negative dose-response — the cells treated with high drug concentrations appear MORE viable than untreated cells. The drug is a kinase inhibitor dissolved in DMSO. All concentrations use 0.1% final DMSO. What could explain a paradoxical increase in MTT signal at high drug concentrations?

expected_root_causes: Drug directly reduces MTT (false positive absorbance)
Drug-induced cell proliferation at sub-toxic doses (hormesis)
MTT crystal precipitation at high drug concentration
Drug interfering with mitochondrial metabolism measurement
